adds.rationale: Opens an explicit robotics-wave slot named in the profile thesis; BOTZ provides diversified exposure to industrial robotics, automation, and AI-adjacent names that complement single-stock NVDA without doubling the AI bucket.

news_evidence.summary: Global X identifies four themes inside BOTZ as of Jan 31 2023: industrial robotics and automation, unmanned vehicles and drones, AI, and non-industrial robotics; over half of holdings outside the US with significant Japan weight.
Industrial-automation and AI-linked equities rallied through H1 2023 as enterprise AI investment accelerated alongside NVDA's data-center surge, broadening the buildout beyond pure GPU names.

removes.rationale: Energy bucket is overweighted (XLE + LNG + URA = 3 of 5 slots); XLE overlaps LNG inside the geopolitical-energy thesis while crude has pulled back sharply (Brent fell ~$16 to ~$73/b between mid-April and early May 2023), eroding the broad-oil tailwind. LNG remains the cleaner Russia-Ukraine LNG-export bet, so dropping XLE frees a slot for the uncovered robotics wave without abandoning the geopolitical-energy thesis.

news_evidence.summary: EIA reports Brent crude fell $16/b to $73/b and WTI fell $15/b to $69/b between April 12 and May 4 2023; agency expects only modest H2 2023 recovery to ~$78-79/b as OPEC+ cuts offset weaker demand.

Cheniere supplied ~70% of its LNG to Europe in 2022, covering ~30% of the Russian pipeline-gas shortfall; the Russia-Ukraine LNG-export thesis is concentrated in LNG, leaving XLE as a redundant broad-oil overlay.

rationale_overall: The watchlist was concentrated 3-of-5 in energy/commodities (XLE, LNG, URA) and had no slot for robotics, one of the profile's named next waves. Swapping XLE for BOTZ keeps the geopolitical-energy thesis intact through LNG (the cleaner Russia-Ukraine export play) and the nuclear thesis intact through URA, while opening a robotics-wave slot to complement NVDA's AI exposure and RKLB's rockets exposure. Post-change watchlist of 5 spans one ticker per wave bucket: AI (NVDA), nuclear (URA), rockets (RKLB), geopolitical-energy (LNG), robotics (BOTZ). Quantum remains uncovered but Q1 2023 revenues at IonQ (~$4M) and Rigetti ($2.2M) are too small to justify displacing an existing position this quarter.
